other_observers: Dick Schottler; found by Joe Roller on the 4th
description: A dark jaeger, with bulky body and head. Chocolate brown mantle in resting bird with no apparent cinnamon tones or barring visible. Head color was medium to light brown contrasting with mantle in resting bird. Base of primaries white, seen well in underwing of flying bird. White at primary shafts of outer few primaries above, seen in flying bird. White at the base of the underwing primary coverts giving a prominent double white flash in the flying bird. Bird appear bulky compared to PAJA and LTJA. A slight central tail feather protrusion was detected.Behaviors: Harassing gulls, while flying from one end of lake to the other, Occasionally resting on water.Call: multiple, rising notes, gull like call as it flew overheadPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek
